synthesis_text: To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, individuals should practice good personal hygiene, including regular hand washing with soap and water [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Wearing a face mask in public and when in close contact with others is also recommended [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Maintaining physical or social distancing can help lower transmission risk [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Ensuring proper ventilation of indoor spaces decreases the likelihood of virus spread [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. It is crucial to stay home if diagnosed with COVID-19 or if you believe you may be infected [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Covering coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow helps prevent the spread of droplets [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Avoiding sharing personal household items minimizes transmission risk [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094]. Staying up to date on COVID-19 vaccinations enhances protection against severe illness [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Keeping unwashed hands away from your face can prevent infection [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00006]. Finally, quarantining if exposed to someone with COVID-19 is essential to prevent further spread [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00129].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to stay up to date on your COVID-19 vaccinations, which helps prevent serious illness [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Ensuring good indoor airflow can also help prevent the spread of the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Practicing physical distancing reduces the risk of transmission [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Wearing a mask in appropriate settings protects both yourself and others [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Maintaining good hygiene by washing hands frequently with soap and water is essential [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Avoiding close contact with anyone who is sick or has symptoms further minimizes risk [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Additionally, covering your coughs and sneezes helps prevent spreading the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Testing for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure is also recommended [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Finally, staying informed about local COVID-19 guidance and recommendations can provide valuable information for prevention [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00029].

To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, individuals should avoid crowded places and practice physical distancing of at least 1 metre [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Wearing a mask in public places where physical distancing is not possible is also recommended [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Frequent hand washing with soap and water or using an alcohol-based hand rub is essential [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Keeping indoor spaces well ventilated can help reduce the spread of the virus [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Staying informed about local COVID-19 guidance and recommendations is crucial [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00029]. Getting vaccinated helps reduce the spread of the virus and the severity of illness [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]. Regularly cleaning and disinfecting frequently touched surfaces is also important [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00026]. Finally, individuals should quarantine if exposed to someone with COVID-19 to prevent further spread [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00129] and stay home if diagnosed or suspect they may be infected [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00094].
